sub_pixel_system.notes: Not documented. Racing sim from 1992 with 25 fps framerate and polygon-based 3D graphics.

special_mechanics.name: Car Physics Engine
special_mechanics.description: Detailed physics simulation modeling tire wear, traction, grip variation based on corner entry and acceleration timing. Responsive to tunable parameters including gear ratios, tire compounds, and wing settings.

special_mechanics.name: Driving Assistance Features
special_mechanics.description: Brake assistance applies brakes automatically for corners. Steering help smoothens digital input for keyboard/joystick control. Throttle assistance prevents wheel spin during acceleration.

special_mechanics.name: Tire Dynamics
special_mechanics.description: Qualifying tires degrade rapidly - can only drive 1-2 laps before significant grip loss and handling degradation. Race tires model progressive wear.

tier_notes: T2 empirical/qualitative data. This is a racing simulator, not a character platformer. Movement parameters (walk, run, jump, gravity) are not applicable. Core physics focus is on vehicle dynamics, tire grip, acceleration/deceleration responsiveness, and tunable car setup. No sub-pixel precision data documented for driving physics.
sources_used: Wikipedia - Formula One Grand Prix (video game)
